The spec uses an existential quantifier over 4 booleans (each bag goes to friend 1 or friend 2).\n\nThe method computes the result by checking all possible ways to split 4 items into two groups:\n- `a1 + a2 == a3 + a4` (bags 1,2 vs 3,4)\n- `a1 + a3 == a2 + a4` (bags 1,3 vs 2,4)\n\n\n- `a1 + a4 == a2 + a3` (bags 1,4 vs 2,3)\n- `a1 + a2 + a3 == a4` (bags 1,2,3 vs 4)\n- `a1 + a2 + a4 == a3` (bags 1,2,4 vs 3)\n- `a1 + a3 + a4 == a2` (bags 1,3,4 vs 2)\n- `a2 + a3 + a4 == a1` (bags 2,3,4 vs 1)\n\nThere are 16 possible assignments of bags to friends, but since flipping all choices gives the same balance condition, I really have 8 unique partitions to check. The all-false and all-true cases are impossible anyway since they'd require either friend 1 or friend 2 to get nothing, which violates the constraint that all bags have weight ≥ 1. Let me think through what the FriendSum function actually computes—it sums up the bags assigned to friend 1 based on the binary choices. I'm working through all 16 possible assignments of which items go to each friend, checking which ones satisfy the equal split condition. For each assignment, I'm determining what constraint it places on the values a1 through a4—like when friend 1 gets a1 and a2 while friend 2 gets a3 and a4, they're equal only if a1+a2=a3+a4. a1+a3, friend2=a2+a4 → a1+a3=a2+a4\n- (T,F,F,T): friend1=a1+a4, friend2=a2+a3 → a1+a4=a2+a3\n- (F,T,T,F): friend1=a2+a3, friend2=a1+a4 → same as a1+a4=a2+a3\n- (F,T,F,T): friend1=a2+a4, friend2=a1+a3 → same as a1+a3=a2+a4\n- (F,F,T,T): friend1=a3+a4, friend2=a1+a2 → same as a1+a2=a3+a4\n- (T,T,T,F): friend1=a1+a2+a3, friend2=a4 → a1+a2+a3=a4\n- (T,T,F,T): friend1=a1+a2+a4, friend2=a3 → a1+a2+a4=a3\n- (T,F,T,T): friend1=a1+a3+a4, friend2=a2 → a1+a3+a4=a2\n- (F,T,T,T): friend1=a2+a3+a4, friend2=a1 → a2+a3+a4=a1\n- (T,T,T,T): friend1=a1+a2+a3+a4, friend2=0 → need total=0\n\nNow I'm looking at the remaining cases where three values go to one friend and one to the other, plus the edge case where all four sum to zero.\n\nSo the unique conditions are these seven equations, and the impossible cases are when all values are false or all true since we need at least one person in each group. Now I need to prove both directions: showing that when the result is true I can provide witnesses for the equal distribution, and that if equal distribution is possible then one of these seven conditions must hold.\n\nFor the forward direction, I'll use explicit assertions to demonstrate the witnesses—for instance, if the first condition holds, I can assert that splitting the group according to that partition produces equal sums. For the reverse, I need Dafny to verify that checking all 16 possible boolean combinations covers every case, which should force any valid distribution into one of the seven conditions.\n\nWriting the proof code... Writing assertion cases... Writing assertion logic... I'm concerned that Dafny might struggle to automatically verify the negative case where no valid assignment exists, even though there are only 16 possible boolean combinations for the solver to check. Let me try the straightforward approach first—providing witnesses for when a distribution works—and see if Dafny can handle proving the impossibility on its own.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":9720,"cache_read_input_tokens":8610,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":9720},"output_tokens":14,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5951e490-56fa-4bcb-b092-0f04033a68ef","uuid":"ef895cbc-1251-4f9e-940e-da00a9523147"},{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01LWwf8VARLA7oBzyQDdkb6j","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Looking at this problem, there are no loops - the challenge is proving the equivalence between the computed boolean expression and the existential quantifier `CanDistributeEqually`. function FriendSum(a1: int, a2: int, a3: int, a4: int,\n                   b1: bool, b2: bool, b3: bool, b4: bool): int\n{\n  (if b1 then a1 else 0) + (if b2 then a2 else 0) +\n  (if b3 then a3 else 0) + (if b4 then a4 else 0)\n}\n\nghost predicate CanDistributeEqually(a1: int, a2: int, a3: int, a4: int)\n{\n  exists b1: bool, b2: bool, b3: bool, b4: bool ::\n    FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, b1, b2, b3, b4) ==\n    FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, !b1, !b2, !b3, !b4)\n}\n\nmethod DawidAndCandies(a1: int, a2: int, a3: int, a4: int) returns (result: bool)\n  requires a1 >= 1 && a2 >= 1 && a3 >= 1 && a4 >= 1\n  ensures result == CanDistributeEqually(a1, a2, a3, a4)\n{\n  result := (a1 + a2 == a3 + a4) || (a1 + a3 == a2 + a4) || (a1 + a4 == a2 + a3) ||\n            (a1 + a2 + a3 == a4) || (a1 + a2 + a4 == a3) || (a1 + a3 + a4 == a2) || (a2 + a3 + a4 == a1);\n\n  if result {\n    if a1 + a2 == a3 + a4 {\n      assert FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, true, true, false, false) ==\n             FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, false, false, true, true);\n    } else if a1 + a3 == a2 + a4 {\n      assert FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, true, false, true, false) ==\n             FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, false, true, false, true);\n    } else if a1 + a4 == a2 + a3 {\n      assert FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, true, false, false, true) ==\n             FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, false, true, true, false);\n    } else if a1 + a2 + a3 == a4 {\n      assert FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, false, false, false, true) ==\n             FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, true, true, true, false);\n    } else if a1 + a2 + a4 == a3 {\n a4, false, true, false, false) ==\n             FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, true, false, true, true);\n    } else {\n      assert a2 + a3 + a4 == a1;\n      assert FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, true, false, false, false) ==\n             FriendSum(a1, a2, a3, a4, false, true, true, true);\n    }\n  }\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":9720,"cache_read_input_tokens":8610,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":9720},"output_tokens":14,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5951e490-56fa-4bcb-b092-0f04033a68ef","uuid":"486a3cbd-b480-4a4b-98c3-801272a88e30"},{"type":"rate_limit_event","rate_limit_info":{"status":"allowed","resetsAt":1773788400,"rateLimitType":"five_hour","overageStatus":"rejected","overageDisabledReason":"org_level_disabled","isUsingOverage":false},"uuid":"84b67a10-0fc8-428c-b978-b8f8a3e69ac5","session_id":"5951e490-56fa-4bcb-b092-0f04033a68ef"},{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GJF4K6NaghhMhWfg5Mdt2z","type":"tool_result","content":"File
